entry.title: Scrapping process using ADGI (post unplanned goods issue)
entry.content:
Scrapping:

Scrapping in SAP EWM refers to the process of removing damaged or obsolete inventory from the warehouse. This action updates stock levels and ensures that unusable goods are not part of future transactions. SAP EWM allows scrapping to be done efficiently by associating it with movement types and reasons.

 

Post Unplanned Goods Issue:

Posting an unplanned goods issue in SAP EWM occurs when goods are issued from inventory without a prior order or plan. This process helps adjust stock levels and manage unforeseen circumstances like damage or errors. SAP allows tracking of unplanned goods issues to ensure accurate inventory management and reporting.

 

Warehouse Number:

The warehouse number uniquely identifies a specific warehouse within the system. It is used to manage inventory, define storage locations, and configure operational processes. SAP EWM leverages the warehouse number to support various warehouse-specific functions and ensure efficient warehouse management.

 

Storage Type:

Storage type is the classification of physical storage areas within a warehouse, such as shelves, bins, or pallets. It helps organize goods based on their characteristics and requirements, like temperature or size. SAP EWM uses storage types to define the layout of the warehouse and optimize space utilization.

 

Storage Bin:

A storage bin is a specific location within a warehouse used to store goods. It is part of the warehouse structure and is defined by its unique identifier, ensuring accurate tracking of materials. SAP EWM assigns goods to storage bins for efficient stock management and retrieval.

Movement Reason:


Movement reasons are used to specify the cause or purpose of a goods movement, such as transferring, scrapping, or replenishing inventory. It helps track the context behind inventory transactions, ensuring transparency in warehouse operations. SAP EWM provides predefined movement reasons for various processes to support reporting and analysis.

 

Steps to perform Scrapping process using ADGI (post unplanned goods issue)

 

Step 1:

Enter the transaction code /N/SCWM/ADGI

 

 

 

Step 2:

In the post unplanned goods issue screen provide the below details:

  1. Warehouse number
  2. Storage Type
  3. Storage Bin
  4. GI Process
  5. Movement Reason

And Uncheck “Select scarp stock only”

 

Then click on the execute button.

 

 

Step 3:

In the next step we need to post items

Select the line item you wish to scrap and click on the Post GI button.

 

Scrapping is completed.

 

Conclusion:

Scrapping process using ADGI (post unplanned goods issue) is performed.

entry.title: Feature Control in RAP, Dynamic Feature Control, Part - 2
entry.content:
Hello, 
In this blog post we are going to Learn about Dynamic Feature Control in RAP.

With Feature Control, the various fields and properties can be adjusted again at runtime, for example to activate or deactivate actions. In this article you will learn more about how to use this function to get more flexibility in your data model.

This function is an optional feature provided by the RAP Framework, which complements existing functionalities. While it is not a mandatory component, it can be utilized to enhance certain aspects of the application. In the current example, this function has not yet been implemented, but we will explore its usage in this article. Specifically, feature control can be applied to fields to modify or influence their behavior, such as controlling their visibility or editability.

When implementing this feature, you need to decide how to apply it:

  1. Instance-based feature ("features:instance"): The data of individual instances is available, and the feature's behavior is determined for each instance individually.
  2. Global feature ("features:global"): There is no access to instance-specific data, and the decision is made once globally on whether the feature can be used.

Procedure

Here we are defining a Custom table (ZKK_DT_TRAVEL). 

The table zkk_dt_travel is a transparent table in SAP that stores travel-related information. It includes details such as travel ID, agency ID, customer ID, travel dates, booking fees, total price, currency, status and a description. It also tracks the creation and modification details like created by, created at, last changed by, and last changed at. 

 

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Travel table' 
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 
define table zkk_dt_travel { 
key client : abap.clnt not null; 
key travel_id : /dmo/travel_id not null; 
agency_id : /dmo/agency_id; 
customer_id : /dmo/customer_id; 
begin_date : /dmo/begin_date; 
end_date : /dmo/end_date; 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode : '/dmo/travel_m.currency_code' 
booking_fee : abap.curr(16,2); 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode : '/dmo/travel_m.currency_code' 
total_price : /dmo/total_price; 
currency_code : /dmo/currency_code; 
description : /dmo/description; 
status : abap_boolean; 
created_by : abp_creation_user; 
created_at : abp_creation_tstmpl; 
last_changed_by : abp_locinst_lastchange_user; 
last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl; 
}

 

The Above table holds the records which are given below 

For this database table(ZKK_DT_TRAVEL) we are defining the Basic view/Interface view. 

The ZKK_I_TRAVEL is a CDS view based on the zkk_dt_travel  table. It provides a structured way to access travel data, including fields like travel ID, agency ID, customer ID, travel dates, booking fees, total price, currency, and timestamps. It uses annotations to enhance usability, such as semantic tags for amounts, currencies, and metadata for created/changed users and timestamps. 

 

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE] 
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface view for Travelling' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true 
@ObjectModel.usageType:{ 
serviceQuality: #X, 
sizeCategory: #S, 
dataClass: #MIXED 
} 
define root view entity ZKK_I_TRAVEL 
as select from zkk_dt_travel 
{ 
key travel_id as TravelId, 
agency_id as AgencyId, 
customer_id as CustomerId, 
begin_date as BeginDate, 
end_date as EndDate, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode' 
booking_fee as bookingfee, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode' 
total_price as TotalPrice, 
currency_code as CurrencyCode, 
description as Description, 
status as status, 
@Semantics.user.createdBy: true 
created_by as CreatedBy, 
@Semantics.systemDateTime.createdAt: true 
created_at as CreatedAt, 
@Semantics.user.localInstanceLastChangedBy: true 
last_changed_by as LastChangedBy, 
@Semantics.systemDateTime.lastChangedAt: true 
last_changed_at as LastChangedAt 
}

 

 Now I am defining the Projection view on top of interface view. 

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE] 
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'projection view for Travel' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true 
@ObjectModel.usageType:{ 
serviceQuality: #X, 
sizeCategory: #S, 
dataClass: #MIXED 
} 
define root view entity ZKK_C_TRAVEL  
as projection on ZKK_I_TRAVEL as Travel 
{ 
key TravelId, 
AgencyId, 
CustomerId, 
BeginDate, 
EndDate, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode' 
bookingfee, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode' 
TotalPrice, 
CurrencyCode, 
Description, 
status, 
CreatedBy, 
CreatedAt, 
LastChangedBy, 
LastChangedAt 
}

 

 

 Now I am defining the Metadata Extension for projection View. 

 

 

@Metadata.layer: #PARTNER 
annotate entity ZKK_C_TRAVEL with 
{ 
@UI.facet: [{ 
purpose: #STANDARD, 
position: 10, 
type : #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE, 
label: 'Travel Details' 
}] 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 10, label: 'Travel Id' }] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'Travel Id' }] 
TravelId; 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 20, label: 'Agency Id' }] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'Agency Id' }] 
AgencyId; 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 30, label: 'Customer Id' }] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'Customer Id' }]
CustomerId; 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 40, label: 'Begin Date' }] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'Begin Date' }] 
BeginDate; 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 50, label: 'End Date' }] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'End Date' }] 
EndDate; 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 60, label: 'Booking Fee' }] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'Booking Fee' }] 
bookingfee; 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 70, label: 'Total Price' }] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'Total Price' }] 
TotalPrice; 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 80, label: 'Status Confirm' }, 
{ type: #FOR_ACTION, dataAction: 'status', label: 'Status Confirm' } ] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'Status' }, 
{ type: #FOR_ACTION, dataAction: 'status', label: 'Status' }] 
status; 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 100, label: 'Currency' }] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'Currency' }] 
CurrencyCode; 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 90, label: 'Description' }] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'Description' }] 
Description; 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 110, label: 'Created By' }] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'Created By' }] 
CreatedBy; 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 120, label: 'Created At' }] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'Created At' }] 
CreatedAt; 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 130, label: 'Last Changed By' }] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'Last Changed By' }] 
LastChangedBy; 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 140, label: 'Last Changed At' }] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ label: 'Last Chnaged at' }] 
LastChangedAt; 
}

 

 Now I am defining the Behavior Definition for Interface/Basic view. 

 

managed implementation in class zbp_kk_i_travel unique; 
strict ( 2 ); 
define behavior for ZKK_I_TRAVEL alias Travel //alias <alias_name> 
persistent table zkk_dt_travel 
lock master 
authorization master ( instance ) 
//etag master <field_name> 
{ 
create; 
update; 
delete; 
field ( readonly : update ) TravelId; 
field ( readonly ) LastChangedAt, LastChangedBy, CreatedAt, CreatedBy; 
field ( mandatory : create ) AgencyId, CustomerId, BeginDate, EndDate, bookingfee, CurrencyCode; 
action ( features : instance ) status result [1] $self; 
mapping for ZKK_DT_TRAVEL { 
TravelId = travel_id; 
AgencyId = agency_id; 
CustomerId = customer_id; 
BeginDate = begin_date; 
EndDate = end_date; 
bookingfee = booking_fee; 
TotalPrice = total_price; 
CurrencyCode = currency_code; 
Description = description; 
status = status; 
CreatedBy = created_by; 
CreatedAt = created_at; 
LastChangedBy = last_changed_by; 
LastChangedAt = last_changed_at; 
} 
}

 

Create the Behavior Definition for Projection view. 

 

projection; 
//strict ( 2 ); 
define behavior for ZKK_C_TRAVEL alias Travel //alias <alias_name> 
{ 
use create; 
use update; 
use delete; 

use action status; 
}

 

Instance feature

Here in behavior definition of interface view we have to use Feature control with Actions. 

 After this we have to implement 2 methods, Status and get_instance_feature method. 

Here is the class. 

 

CLASS lhc_ZKK_I_TRAVEL DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS get_instance_authorizations FOR INSTANCE AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_authorizations FOR travel RESULT result.

    METHODS status FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION travel~status RESULT result.

    METHODS get_instance_features FOR INSTANCE FEATURES
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_features FOR travel RESULT result.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_ZKK_I_TRAVEL IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD get_instance_authorizations.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD status.

  MODIFY ENTITIES OF zkk_i_travel IN LOCAL MODE
  ENTITY Travel UPDATE FIELDS ( status )
  WITH VALUE #( for key in keys ( %tky = key-%tky status = abap_true ) )
  FAILED failed
  REPORTED reported.


  read ENTITIES OF  zkk_i_travel IN LOCAL MODE
  ENTITY Travel ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
  RESULT data(travel_data).

  result = VALUE #( for travel_record in travel_data
                  ( %tky = travel_record-%tky %param = travel_record )
                  ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_instance_features.
  read ENTITIES OF zkk_i_travel IN LOCAL MODE
  ENTITY Travel
  FIELDS ( status ) WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
  RESULT data(confirmed_status)
  FAILED failed.

  result = VALUE #( for trav in confirmed_status
             let con_status = cond #( when trav-status = abap_true
                                      THEN if_abap_behv=>fc-o-disabled
                                      else if_abap_behv=>fc-o-enabled  )


                                      IN ( %tky = trav-%tky
                                      %action-status = con_status
                                      )
                                      ).

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

 

Implement the method Status with functionality triggered by the button. This method can include any business logic based on the requirements. In the current scenario, it sets the field value to True. 

The method GET_INSTANCE_FEATURES has been implemented to manage the control states (Enable or Disable in the current scenario). If the Status Confirmed is Yes then The Status button will be Disabled. 

Run Application. 

Scenario: If the Status Confirmed is ‘NO’ then we can change it to ‘Yes’, Using Confirm Status Button on list page. If Status Confirmed is ‘Yes’ then the button will be Disable. 

As we can see the status in above image is No, if we select the particular field the Confirm Status will be enabled. 

If we Confirm the status then the value will become ‘YES’ 

When we select the fields with Status ‘YES’ the button will be in disables State. 

Conclusion

Dynamic Feature Control

Dynamic feature control allows for runtime adjustments based on specific business logic or conditions. This is implemented using RAP methods like GET_INSTANCE_FEATURES. It enables controls to adapt based on context, such as user roles, permissions, or the status of the business object. For instance, the "Approve" button might be enabled only for records in the "Pending Approval" state, or a "Cancel" button could become inactive after the travel date has passed.

entry.title: Integrating Business Event Logging with SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Integrating Business Event Logging

This blog explores various aspects of integrating Business Event Logging (BEL) with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, focusing on data models, integration scenarios, and the use of SAP Business Technology Platform.

BEL Data Model

In the data model of Business Event Logging, we store the following essential information in an event header for each Business Event:

  • Event type (Object type + operation)
  • Object keys
  • The user that executed the underlying action
  • Timestamp of the action/event creation
  • TCode of the transaction (Available in OP from S/4HANA OP 2023 FPS3)
  • Transaction ID which represents the technical session in which an event was created. This helps   identify the events that were created in the same session.

 Data dependent on the Business Event:

  • Field Changes (Fieldname, new and old value, as contained in the business event)
  • (Optionally) All Business Event Data of the business event. This is stored as a JSON string. You can choose this option in the configuration UI, where you activate business event logging.

We offer a data extraction view supporting full load and delta handling (via CDC):

  • Event Header - C_BusEvtLogEventDEX_2
  • Field Changes - C_BusEvtLogPayloadDEX_2
  • Complete Business EventData JSON format - C_BuEvLgEvtFullPayloadJSONDEX (OP from S/4HANA OP 2023 FPS2)

Integration and Communication Scenarios in BEL

You can connect SAP Signavio Process Intelligence with Business Event Logging (as part of SAP S/4HANA or SAP BTP, ABAP Environment) either directly or via SAP Datasphere (default option).

Depending on the product, you use different communication scenarios to extract or access business events, field changes, or full business event data.

Communication scenarios in SAP BTP, ABAP Environment (2411 or higher):

  • : For data federation or data extraction, or 
  • (Generic) SAP_COM_0532: For data replication. Use the schema API_BusEvtLogEvent with this communication scenario.

Communication scenarios in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition (CE 2411 or higher)

  • SAP_COM_0531 Or , or
  • SAP_COM_0532: For data replication from Business Event Logging to target systems.

Use the following CDS views with SAP_COM_0531 and SAP_COM_0532 scenarios:

  • Business Event Header Data (v2):  C_BusEvtLogEventDEX_2
  • Business Event Property Changes (v2): C_BusEvtLogPayloadDEX_2
  • Business Event Data: C_BuEvLgEvtFullPayloadJSONDEX

Alternatively for SAP_COM_0532 you can also use the schema API_BusEvtLogEvent.

For more information on SAP_COM_0531 see Integrating CDI
For more information on SAP_COM_0532, see Integrating CDS Views Using SAP Datasphere and Integrating SQL Services Using SAP Datasphere

Need to Know for SAP S/4HANA (On-Premise)/Cloud Private Edition

SAP Signavio can be connected to SAP S/4HANA On-Premise either directly or via SAP Datasphere.

  1. Integration via SAP Datasphere (SAP S/4HANA – SAP Datasphere – SAP Signavio)
    Using Cloud Connector SAP S/4HANA On-Premise system can be connected to SAP Datasphere. The Cloud Connector serves as a link between applications in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP Datasphere) and on-premise or private cloud systems (SAP S/4HANA), enabling the use of existing on-premise assets without exposing the entire internal landscape.
  2. b)     Direct integration, SAP S/4HANA On-Premise – SAP Signavio

Using on-premises extractor, on-premises source system can be connected to Process Data Management in SAP Signavio.

Connect SAP Datasphere or SAP Signavio Process Intelligence directly using these views for data extraction:

  • Business Event Header Data (v2): C_BusEvtLogEventDEX_2
  • Business Event Property Changes (v2): C_BusEvtLogPayloadDEX_2
  • Business Event Data: C_BuEvLgEvtFullPayloadJSONDEX

Additional links/general blogs regarding on-premise/private cloud integration are listed below:

  • Consume CDS Views from SAP S/4 HANA on-premise into SAP Data Warehouse Cloud, leveraging the ABAP Pipeline Engine (APE)
  • How to use SAP Data Intelligence with SAP Cloud Connector
  • Preparing Cloud Connector Connectivity
  • Prepare Connectivity to SAP S/4HANA On-Premise

Usage of SAP Datasphere

To leverage the replication capabilities in SAP Datasphere, follow these steps:

  1. Create a Connection
  2. Create a New Replication Flow
  3. Select the CDS or the Schema/table In Load Type, choose Initial and Delta for delta replication or Initial Only for the initial load.
  4. Add/Rename a target table. Create and deploy tables to generate it.
    1. Use SAP HANA on Datasphere as target and use the SAP HANA Connector in Signavio to retrieve the data
    2. Use the new SAP Datasphere Replication Flow Connector to directly pass the data from source system to SAP Signavio (currently in BETA)

For more information, see Integrating CDS Views Using SAP Datasphere.

entry.title: Step by Step Implementation of Secure Login Service for SAP GUI - BTP SSO
entry.content:
Purpose of this blog : 

I have explained the steps for configuring Secure Login Service for SAP GUI - BTP SSO with IAS as identity provider. Please make sure to go through the final notes as well.

Steps to be followed : 

  • Activate the SLS Service in BTP cockpit

For activation of Service, use the guide : HELPGUIDE 

  • Go to Security - > Trust Configuration in BTP cockpit. Complete the trust setup between IAS and BTP cockpit.

Refer to : https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-btp-neo-environment/setting-up-trust-between-identity-authentication-and-sap-btp

 

  • Go to Security -> Role collection. Create the below collections for the roles in BTP cockpit and assign it to the user.  In IAS tenant, go to user&authorisation -> Groups. Create as below and assign to the respective administrator user.

  • Launch the Secure Login Service application and define the SNC User name Suffix

Make sure you enter the right Client policy group host. Use the base URL of the Secure Login Service tenant in it. 

  • Add the SAP Cloud Root CA certificate to the SNC SAPCryptolib in STRUST tcode

Download SAP CLOUD Root CA cert : http://www.pki.co.sap.com/

  • Export both SAP Cloud Root CA certificate and Own certificate from the STRUST's SNC SAPCryptolib and install it in Windows(Local Machine) of all the SSO users.

  • In IAS tenant , go the application-> Secure login service. Go to the Subject name identifier setting and set the primary attribute as EMAIL.

Note : Make sure to use Email in IAS and S4hana's SNC as well.

  • In addition to above 7th point, the Conditional Authentication setting can be set to Corporate Identity provider if in case you are using Azure or etc as AD. In our case, we set it to Identity Authentication as IAS was our primary identity provider.

 

  • Install Secure Login Client with version 3.0.2.21 and make sure your GUI is in latest version as well.
  • Maintain the following Windows Registry entries in the devices of all SSO users.

[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\SAP\SecureLogin\applications\CLOUD-APPLICATION ]

"GSSTargetName"="*"   

"profile"="CLOUD-LOGIN"

"allowFavorite"=dword:00000000

 

[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\SAP\SecureLogin\profiles\CLOUD-LOGIN]

"profileName"="SLS"

"pseType"="browser"

"enrollURL0"="https://<base domain url of sls>/slc/v1/login"

"sslHostCommonNameCheck"=dword:00000000

"sslHostAlternativeNameCheck"=dword:00000001

"showErrorMsg"=dword:00000001

"useWindowsHttpProxy"="dword:00000001

  • Once registry is made, restart your device. You should be seeing a profile entry in Secure login client. If note, please add it like the below.
  • Make the SNC entry in SU01 for all users. Use the transaction SNC1 for mass maintenance of SNC in su01 for all users.

 

Format : p:CN= email, L=<IAS tenant url>, OU=cf-us20-secure-login-service, OU=SAP BTP Clients, O=SAP SE, C=DE

  • Go the GUI entry settings and add the SNC entry.

Tip : use the same name as STRUST own certificate in this above step with "p:" 

  • Now try the GUI SSO.

 

Note :

1. To avoid issue with existing Kerberos SSO, set the "GSSTargetName" registry entry only for SNC's of systems which will be used with the BTP SSO.

entry.title: Not Possible to Not Assign a Down Payment Request in an Invoice Request
entry.content:
The down payment request created through a sales order cannot be viewed in the General tab of the outbound delivery invoice request under the Assigned Down Payments section.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Not Possible to Not Assign a Down Payment Request in...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

If a down payment request has been created via a sales order, you will not be able to see the assignment in the General tab of the outbound delivery invoice request under the section Assigned Down Payments because the system does not check the pricing details but the system will automatically transfer the down payment to the invoice as the outbound delivery request is based on the sales order for which the down payment request was created.

You can refer to the KBA 2013735 - Not Possible to Not Assign a Down Payment Request in an Invoice Request" to understand the system behavior better. 

Hope this clarifies your query. 

 

Thanks & Regards,

Arjun T H

SAP Product Support

entry.title: Re: IPS Trasformation add Org Member Roles
entry.content: That was helpful. The error is gone, but no org members are written User Read Read 1 User Write Skipped 1 I am also using the default transformation. I pinned the group condition as a test. "group": { "condition": '('%btp.cf.pm.group.prefix%' === 'null') || ($.displayName =~ /%btp.cf.pm.group.prefix%%btp.cf.pm.landscape%.+/)", "mappings": [.... I did not set the %btp.cf.pm.group.prefix% property. %btp.cf.pm.group.prefix% then this is null My IAS group is called BTP_CF_PM_12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789101112_org_manager Org ID= 12345678-1234-1234-1234-1234-123456789101112 Pattern BTP_CF_PM_ORGID_ORGRole ias.group.filter=displayName eq "BTP_CF_PM_".

entry.title: Comparing UiPath Impact Analysis and Tricentis LiveCompare: Key Differences
entry.content:
Overview of UiPath Impact Analysis

UiPath Heatmap for SAP is a feature of the UiPath Test Suite designed to optimize test prioritization based on real-time SAP system usage. It leverages data-driven insights to identify automation opportunities and align test efforts with critical business processes.

Key Features:

  • Real-Time Process Insights: Visualizes SAP transactions and their usage frequency to prioritize testing.

  • Test Coverage Visualization: Displays test coverage through color-coded indicators:

    • Gray: No test case exists or has been executed.

    • Red: Test case exists but has failed.

    • Green: Test case exists and has passed.

  • Immediate Results: Requires minimal setup, offering instant insights to identify gaps in test coverage and critical risks.

  • Integration: Compatible with SAP ECC, S/4HANA, and SAP EWM.

Strengths:

  • Prioritizes test cases based on real system usage.

  • Simple and intuitive visualization for test management.

  • Seamless integration with UiPath’s low-code automation platform.

  • Focus on test optimization and automation scalability.

2. Overview of Tricentis LiveCompare

Tricentis LiveCompare is a comprehensive impact analysis and testing tool designed specifically for SAP environments. It provides AI-driven insights into system changes, helping organizations minimize risks and optimize testing efforts.

Key Features:

  • Change Impact Analysis: Uses AI-powered analytics to identify SAP objects at risk from transports, support packs, or change requests.

  • Continuous Configuration Analysis: Automatically identifies configuration-related changes affecting master data and functional testing.

  • Code Quality Monitoring: Monitors and analyzes custom ABAP code for quality and dependency issues.

  • Smart Project Guides: Guides SAP teams through quality activities at each stage of a project, aligned with SAP Activate.

  • Role-Based UI: Provides tailored analytics and reports based on user roles.

Strengths:

  • Comprehensive impact analysis for SAP system updates.

  • Focus on risk minimization and testing efficiency.

  • Advanced AI and analytics for deep insights into changes.

  • Detailed reports for test prioritization and audit readiness.

3. UiPath Platform vs. Tricentis Tosca

Feature Comparison:

FeatureUiPath PlatformTricentis Tosca

Process LibraryStrong capabilities (9.6), offering efficient test management.Equally rated at 9.6, focusing on managing test cases effectively.
Execution ManagementSuperior (9.5), excelling in automated workflow management.Slightly behind, with less emphasis on execution management.
Test FeedbackDelivers actionable insights (9.2) for automation testing.Provides valuable insights (8.3), though less detailed.
CustomizationHighly flexible (9.3) for tailored solutions.Limited flexibility (8.0) for customization.
Ease of UseStreamlined bot creation process (9.3).User-friendly visual editor (9.1).
Quality of SupportStrong support (8.8), offering responsive assistance.Decent support (8.3), less robust.
Integration CapabilitiesExtensive pre-built connectors (9.2) for seamless app integration.API Designer rated lower (8.0) and seen as somewhat limited.

4. Key Differences Between UiPath Heatmap and Tricentis LiveCompare

FeatureUiPath Heatmap for SAPTricentis LiveCompare
Primary FocusReal-time test prioritization and coverage visualization.Change impact analysis and risk identification for SAP systems.
VisualizationHeatmap with color-coded indicators for test coverage and transaction usage.Detailed impact analysis dashboards and reports.
Technology ApproachData-driven insights from system usage.AI and dependency-based analysis of system changes.
Setup and UsabilityMinimal setup with immediate results.Requires configuration for change analysis.
IntegrationPart of the UiPath ecosystem for automation.Standalone tool integrated with Tricentis Tosca.
Target AudienceTest teams looking to optimize automation coverage.Organizations focused on minimizing risk and managing change impacts.
Test PrioritizationBased on real-time system usage and transaction relevance.Based on changes in code, configuration, and dependencies.
ScopeOptimizes ongoing testing efforts with actionable insights.Focuses on preparing for SAP upgrades and major changes.

5. Which Tool Should You Choose?

Choose UiPath Heatmap for SAP if:

  • You prioritize visual, data-driven test optimization.

  • You already use UiPath’s automation tools and want seamless integration.

  • Your focus is on enhancing automation efficiency and real-time prioritization.

  • You need a tool that delivers immediate insights without complex setup.

Choose Tricentis LiveCompare if:

  • You require a robust solution for managing SAP system changes.

  • Your organization prioritizes risk mitigation during SAP upgrades and migrations.

  • You need detailed impact analysis to identify high-risk areas.

  • You are looking for advanced AI-driven insights and reporting capabilities.

 

6. Conclusion

Both UiPath Heatmap for SAP and Tricentis LiveCompare provide valuable functionalities to support SAP testing and change management. The UiPath Platform shines in its execution management and actionable feedback, while Tricentis Tosca excels in visual editing and ease of use. Meanwhile, UiPath Heatmap excels in real-time test prioritization, and Tricentis LiveCompare offers advanced change impact analysis and risk-based recommendations. Organizations should assess their specific testing needs, existing tools, and long-term goals to select the most suitable solution for their SAP landscape.

entry.title: Consume local OData Service within same backend system
entry.content:
Introduction:

ABAP Restful Application Programming is an efficient and cloud-compatible development model that enables rapid creation of Fiori apps.

As a part of clean core guidelines, it is a best practice to used SAP released APIs to fetch the data within S4HANA system instead of directly querying on unreleased CDS views. 

Implementation

  • Create a local proxy instance to consume OData Version 2 'API_MATERIAL_STOCK_SRV' service.

 

 

DATA(lo_client_proxy)  = 
/iwbep/cl_cp_client_proxy_fact=>create_v2_local_proxy( VALUE #( service_id = 'API_MATERIAL_STOCK_SRV' service_version = '0001'  ) ) .

 

 

  • Create an instance of the entity list resource for entity set 'A_MatlStkInAcctMod'.

 

 

DATA(lo_entity_list_resource) = lo_client_proxy->create_resource_for_entity_set( 'A_MatlStkInAcctMod' ).

 

 

  • Create the read list request instance.

 

 

DATA(lo_read_list_request) = lo_entity_list_resource->create_request_for_read( ).

 

 

  • Call the method set_orderby of read list request to sort in the requested order.

 

 

          DATA(lt_sort_order) = CORRESPONDING /iwbep/if_cp_runtime_types=>ty_t_sort_order( it_sort_order MAPPING property_path = element_name ).
          lo_read_list_request->set_orderby( lt_sort_order  ).

 

 

  • Create the filter factory instance and pass the filter object to request instance to fetch the data based on filters requested. 

 

 

 DATA(lo_filter_factory) = lo_read_list_request->create_filter_factory( ).
    DATA: lo_filter_node_final TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_cp_filter_node.
    LOOP AT it_filter ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lv_filter>).
      TRY.
          DATA(lo_filter_node) = io_filter_factory->create_by_range( iv_property_path = <lv_filter>-name it_range = <lv_filter>-range ).
          IF lo_filter_node_final IS BOUND .
            lo_filter_node_final = lo_filter_node_final->and( lo_filter_node ).
          ELSE .
            lo_filter_node_final = lo_filter_node .
          ENDIF. .
        CATCH /iwbep/cx_gateway.
          "handle exception
      ENDTRY.
    ENDLOOP.
    data(lo_filter) = lo_filter_node_final .
lo_read_list_request->set_filter( lo_filter ) .​

 

 

  • Implement the paging by setting the top and skip to request instance.

 

 

      IF lv_top > 0 .
          lo_read_list_request->set_skip( lv_skip ) .
          lo_read_list_request->set_top( lv_top ) .
        ENDIF.

 

 

  • Execute the read list request and gain the read list response instance:

 

 

     DATA(lo_read_list_response) = lo_read_list_request->execute( ).

 

 

  • Fetch the business data from the response object.

 

 

  lo_read_list_response->get_business_data( IMPORTING et_business_data = et_results ).

 

In this way all the other CRUD operations can be handled using local instance and HTTP client destination configuration can be avoided.

entry.title: SAP BTP Cloud Integration Code search tool
entry.content:
We have looked into SAP documentation and SAP recently recommended using ${body} instead of ${in.body}. In order to identify which artifacts are using this code in an efficient time, especially since we don't yet have a full Git pipeline. (~500 iflows in our current Integration Suite CI capability). We implemented the search code tool. By specifying ${in.body} as the search parameter, the tool scans all iFlows, identifies where this string is used, and returns the names of the relevant iFlows. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/using-camel-simple-expression-language

This tool is having below features:

  • To search a specific code in:
    • Script collection
    • Resources
    • Search function name in message mapping when the iflow uses folder .mmap
  • This tool can't search in function libraries types

High level flow:

 

This tool needs to support multiple packages

Trigger process for this tool execution is based on start timer.

Prerequisites:

  1. Cloud integration credentials:
    1. Create role collection searchCodeiflows and add roles
    2. Create user and assign role connection searchCodeiflows
    3. This user credential is being use to get information about script collection, resources, etc.

To be able to use the tool,

  1. Download the SearchCodeTool project from the BTP-Integration-Suite repository: https://github.com/FatimaMel/BTP-Integration-Suite 
    1. Create a searchCodeTool.zip folder with those files inside:
      1.  
  2. In SAP integration suite, upload the searchCodeTool.zip folder as type Integration Flow in a package
  3. Create in security material:
    1. User Credentials created earlier at step 1.b in Prerequises
      1.  
  4. In configuration of the artifact, set the parameters:
    1. choosePackagesId is for searching in specific packages. Ex: packageId1||packageId2
    2. CIDesignTime with the domain url of the deployment iflow, but remove '-rt' from the url
    3. codeToSearch is the specific code to search
    4. searchCodeUserCred is the name of User Credentials created at step 3.a
    5. SearchInResourcesAndMapping and SearchInScriptCollection are for searching in specific areas. If no value is specified, it will be set to true. 
  5. Performance of the iflow:
    1. The performance depends on the number of packages and integration flow. For about 50 packages and around 350 iflows, it can take up to 7 minutes.

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide: Sending SAP Data to SAP Event Mesh in Integration Suite (EMIS). Codeless.
entry.content:
Overview: 

With the release of REST Gateway as part of SAP Event Mesh in Integration Suite (EMIS), SAP events from the SAP NetWeaver Event Enablement Add-On ("ASAPIO") can now seamlessly flow into EMIS—all through simple configuration, without writing any code.

This integration solution enables us to define exactly which tables and data should be sent in real-time to EMIS. Operating on an event-driven architecture, it allows us to specify the precise business events we want to react to, such as changes in transactional or master data.

In this example, we configure the system to trigger an event whenever a purchase requisition is modified, ensuring that relevant data is instantly sent to EMIS for further processing or integration.

 

Prerequisites:  

  • NetWeaver Add-On for Event Enablement is installed.
  • SAP Integration Suite Service is available. For more information, see “Initial Setup of SAP Integration Suite”.
  • Further information on EMIS: Meet your new friend EMIS: Event Mesh in SAP Integ... - SAP Community. 

 

1. Create RFC-Destinations 

1.1 Create RFC-Destination Connection SAP to SAP Event Mesh Integration Suite. 

To get the Host information of the SAP Integration Suite, Event Mesh Service:  

Step 1. In your BTP Cockpit, navigate to the Subaccount that contains the Integration Suite instance.

Step 2. Go to Instances and Subscriptions. 

Step 3. Under the Instances tab, select the Event Mesh Service instance by clicking the arrow next to it. 

 

Step 4. Click the View button for the Service Key to display the URI, which is located next to the protocol: httprest tag. The URI contains both the Host and Port for the RFC destination. 

Note: Ensure that you enter the host without the "https://" prefix and the ":<Port>" postfix. 

 

Logon and Security  

In the “Security Options” tab, set “SSL” to “Active” and select “ANONYM SSL Client (Anonymous)”. Leave the other entries as they are by default. 

 

Add Certificate to Trust Store   

A browser-specific way to obtain the certificate is to enter the HOST URL from SM59 into the browser and download the root certificate from there. 

After that, you can upload the certificate into SAP using the following steps: 

  1. Transaction: STRUST 
  2. Select the Certificate List used in the RFC destination created earlier. 
  3. Click the "Import Certificate" button. 
  4. Click the "Add to Certificate List" button. 

 

1.2 Token Destination 

Create the destination for the token in the same way as described above. 

In the Target System Settings section, enter the Host for the Access Token of the target system. This can be found in the Service Key of the instance from the SAP Integration Suite Event Mesh Service. 

Enter the postfix "/oauth/token" as the value in the Path Prefix field. 

 

 Logon and Security  

Here you can keep the default settings. 

 

2.0 Create Outbound Interface. 

2.1 Activate BC-Set 

Business Configuration sets (BC-Set) contain customizing and configuration-related table entries that are not imported with the add-on. 

  • Transaction: SCPR20 
  • BC-Set includes: 
  • Configuration for cloud adapter 
  • Configuration for cloud codepages 
  • Definition of IDoc segments 
  • Activate the BC-Set with default values: /ASADEV/ACI_BCSET_FRAMEWORK_SEM 

 

2.1 Create Payload Design 

Go to transaction /n/ASADEV/DESIGN. 

Here, we will define the data that we later want to send to the SAP Event Mesh Integration Suite and use in the Payload Design of our Outbound Object. 

Go to the button Join Builder and insert tables from which you later want to send data. 

Here in this example, we inserted table EBAN and EBKN. 

 

2.2 Create Message Type 

In the next step we will add a message type which we first create in transaction WE81. 

After that we activate the change pointers for that in transaction BD50. 

 

2.3 Connection and Replication Object Customizing 

Navigate to transaction SPRO --> IMG --> SAP NetWeaver AddOn for Event enablement --> Connection and Replication Object Customizing. 

 

 

2.4 Create Connection 

Create a new connection by clicking the "New Entries" button at the top. Then, enter an Instance Name, select the created RFC Destination (Upload) from the Search Help, and choose the ISO-Code and Cloud Type as you can see below in the screenshot. 

 

Default Values 

For the "Default Values", enter the following: 

  • "SAP_EM_CLIENT_ID": This is the Client ID from the SAP Integration Suite Event Mesh Service Key. 
  • "SAP_EM_TOKEN_DESTINATION": This is the destination created in the "Token Destination" chapter for the token. 

 

Outbound Object 

  • Create a New Outbound Object. 
  • Add a new entry and specify the following details: 
    • Object: The name of the outbound configuration. 
    • Extraction Func. Module: /ASADEV/ACI_GEN_PDVIEW_EXTRACT 
    • Message Type: The message type you created earlier. 
    • Load Type: Incremental Load. 
    • Trace: Activate for testing purposes. 
    • Formatting Func.: /ASADEV/ACI_GEN_VIEW_FORM_CB 
    • Field Payload View Name: Name of the payload. 

 

Event Linkage 

Link the Outbound Object to the BOR BUS2105 (Purchase Requisition) and the CHANGED event. 

 

Header Attributes 

  • Add the required Header Attributes, including SAP_EM_TOPIC, to specify where the data will be sent. 

Now that the Outbound Object is set up, it is ready to be used. 

Go to transaction ME52, make a change, and save. 

 

3.0 Test and Monitor the Message 

After making changes, monitor the system to verify if the message is successfully sent. 

Go to transaction /n/ASADEV/ACI_MONITOR. 

On the left side, you can see the JSON message that was sent. It contains all the tables and fields we defined in our Payload Design. On the right side, we can see the response from EMIS

Watch the events being published to the SAP Event Mesh Integration Suite (EMIS). 

 

 Summary:

Sending data to EMIS doesn't require coding with the NetWeaver Add-on for Event Enablement, and this can be done easily with many other objects in SAP through configuration. Additionally, the payload is completely flexible and can be defined in a way that ensures you get exactly the relevant data. Please leave your thoughts in the comments.

entry.title: Re: disp-work, Dispatcher, GRAY, Stopped, , ,10102

Hello @mamartins ,

thank you very much. With your help I was able to fix my problem.

If someone encounters the same problem. Just follow as he says:

  • Go to your licenses https://me.sap.com/licensekey
  • Add your HANA DB as a new system within any installation (the installation doesnt matter)
  • IMPORTANT: Select "SAP HANA, platform edition" I took ... 2.0
  • Then as a license type I used SAP in memory application platform
  • As quantity I entered the maximum, since the SAP Note https://me.sap.com/notes/2957288 says "Alternatively, you could simply set it to a ridiculously high number"
  • after that I generated the key and downloaded it.
  • With this key I just followed the steps in SAP Note https://me.sap.com/notes/0002690863
  • And after that my SAP HANA System started again.

entry.title: SAP IBP Configuration - Create a Compound Master Data Type in SAP IBP
entry.content:
A compound master data type in SAP IBP is a higher-level master data type that combines multiple individual master data types. This allows businesses to manage complex, hierarchical business structures more efficiently. Here are some key points:

  • Combination of Master Data Types: It integrates two or more master data types, such as "Customer" and "Product," to create a comprehensive view of their relationship.
  • Validation and Planning: It validates the data of the included master data types and defines the combinations of planning attributes that can be planned.
  • Holistic Analysis: By combining master data types, businesses can perform more effective analyses, such as identifying customer preferences for specific products.

For example, a compound master data type called "CustomerProduct" could combine "Customer" and "Product" master data types to analyze which products are sold to which customers.

In this article, it documented how to create a compound master data type in SAP IBP.

  1. Choose the Master Data Types tile on the SAP Fiori launchpad home page in the Model Configuration group.

2. Choose New->Compound to create compound master data type on the Master Data Types page.

3. Enter ID, Name and Description, for example, to create compound master type for Cusotmer Product.

4. Choose Add in the Component Master Data Types screen area to add simple master data types.

5. In the Master Data Types dialog box, search and select master data types.

6. The system automatically selects the key attributes for the selected simple master data types in the Assigned Attributes screen area.

7. Choose Add in the Assigned Attributes screen area to add other attributes.

8. On the bottom right of the screen, choose Save.

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use text chunks and embeddings to improve your RAG response.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Friday, March 7th in Pune, India 🇮🇳 from 9:00 to 16:00 IST. The language of the event and the content will be in English. The instructor is Developer Advocate Nora von Thenen from SAP.

The event is kindly being hosted by Bloomberg Data Services (India) Private Ltd., thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. 

Location : Bloomberg Data Services (India) Private Ltd, SkyOne, S. No. 210, 5th Floor,Kalyani Nagar,                                    Village Yerwada, Pune, IN, 411 006

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: Re: Please advise how to write on WBS Budget Availability...
entry.content:
Dear @GiorgioDep,

this is the answer I received from my colleagues from Project Management:
For Project System the classical budgeting and budget availability control functions are available.
A budget availability control based on ACDOCP similar to the classical budget availability control is currently not available for projects.

To my understanding there are no APIs for BPJA e BPGE, but maybe  RFC KBPP_EXTERN_UPDATE_CO could be evaluated (https://me.sap.com/notes/625613).

Alternative you can also copy data from ACDOCP into classical CO-tables and then use IMCCP3 to transfer the data to budget. However, this certainly requires various copy steps and would not reflect e.g. any changes done to the budget in CJ30 or supplements, returns, transfers back in ACDOCP.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service – Part 4
entry.content:
Hi @carlos_vegasgarca 
please completely go through part 2 of this blog post and especially through the section "Running Standalone". It explains how to do the mappings.

The reuse library does not need an own approuter. It must be served from the same approuter as the using app. Otherwise it would come from a different domain and access would be blocked (unless you activate CORS).

Please note that this blog series is about an old  way how to integrate the apps. There is a never approach, which is described in my new blog series.
One important difference is that in the old approach, you bind the XSUAA to the approuter.
This way, you can only use one XSUAA and the reuse library can't provide an own one. In the new way, you bind the XSUAA to the app-host. This way you can have an own XSUAA for the app-host of the reuse library. You have not provided details for your library MTA, so I can't see whether this is the case.

Regards Matthias

entry.title: Re: PresentationVariants on Object Page Table
entry.content:
Here is the answer that you are looking for

https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/blob/main/06_object_page_content.md#presentation-variant---object-page

child entity:

@UI.presentationVariant: [
  {
    qualifier: 'pVariant',
    maxItems: 5,
    sortOrder: [
      {
        by: 'StringProperty',
        direction: #DESC
      }
    ],
    visualizations: [{type: #AS_LINEITEM}]
  }
]

parent entity:

// Search Term #OPTable, #PresentationVariant-Child
@UI.facet: [
  {
    purpose: #STANDARD,
    type: #PRESENTATIONVARIANT_REFERENCE,
    targetElement: '_Child',
    targetQualifier: 'pVariant',
    label: 'Child Entity (1..n)(#OPTable)',
    id: 'childEntitiesSection'
  }
]

entry.title: Re: Flush method is sap mdg fpm
entry.content:
Hi Karthik,

I guess you have inherited feeder classes. If this is the case and the superclass contains some logic in the flush method, I would consider keeping the code.

If the code in the superclass does not have any effect (for example, it checks for a certain event which you are not using) then you could get rid of it. However, in that case it should not have any effect on the runtime as well.

Cheers

Ingmar

entry.title: 🇩🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Code (Bielefeld, Germany)
entry.content:
This is the all-new and revised SAP CodeJam event for SAP Build low-code/no-code tooling and will give you a hands-on experience with SAP Build for building apps, business processes, and business sites without coding. 

You will get to know the environment by creating:

  • A business app for an online store (SAP Build Apps).
  • A business process for approving purchases (SAP Build Process Automation).
  • An apps landing page (SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition)
  • We will also demo desktop automations and a full business site (SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition).

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • Complete these prerequisites BEFORE you come: Set Up Prerequisites for SAP Build CodeJam 

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday, March 18, 2025 from 09:00 - 17:00 local time in Bielefeld, Germany (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English.

entry.title: Re: WB2R_SC - Settlement document that reduces business v...
entry.content:
Hello Marco,

This is possible using additional business volume tables in the profiles for business volume selection for the specified contract. Standard CDS view is VWB2_WBRKWBRP_1. You can use it as a reference. This CDS view selects data from the settlement document header and item tables WBRK/WBRP and overlays it in the corresponding columns in the WB2R_BUSVOL report. As Gauthier said, you may need additional custom logic in this standard CDS view, so you can use it as a reference. To reduce the business volume, you can invert the amounts.

Thanks

entry.title: Email to Purchase order creator with PDF attachment of PO after it is approved in flexible workflow
entry.content:
Requirement is to send the PO creator with PDF attachment of PO after it is completely approved from Flexible workflow. I tried the below options

entry.title: 🇩🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Code (Hamburg, Germany)
entry.content:
This is the all-new and revised SAP CodeJam event for SAP Build low-code/no-code tooling and will give you a hands-on experience with SAP Build for building apps, business processes, and business sites without coding. 

You will get to know the environment by creating:

  • A business app for an online store (SAP Build Apps).
  • A business process for approving purchases (SAP Build Process Automation).
  • An apps landing page (SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition)
  • We will also demo desktop automations and a full business site (SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition).

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • Complete these prerequisites BEFORE you come: Set Up Prerequisites for SAP Build CodeJam 

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Monday, March 17, 2025 from 09:00 - 15:30 local time in Hamburg, Germany (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: SAP Schulungszentrum Hamburg, Tesdorpfstraße 8, 20148 Hamburg, Germany, DE 

  • The instructor is SAP Developer Advocate @Dan_Wroblewski.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 20 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right.

We look forward to welcoming you at the SAP MaxAttention Summit 2025, taking place April 13-15 in Munich, Germany. 

From Sunday evening through Tuesday afternoon, the summit will feature captivating keynotes from SAP executives, customer experience sessions, and in-depth expert discussions. In addition, there will be a variety of networking opportunities, including a welcome reception, a special evening event, and one-on-one meetings.

entry.title: SAP CAP(Java) cds infix filter
entry.content:
Hi!

Looking for support about infix filters in cds in a SAP CAP Java project. I have the following entity structure (simplified view):

entiy ![Case] : cuid, managed {
roles : Association to many RoleUserMap
on roles.caseID = $self;
}
entity RoleUserMap : cuid, managed {
caseID : Association to one ![Case];
roleName : RoleType;
userIDExternal : String(100);
user : Association to one User;
}

@CDS.persistence.skip
entity User {
key ID : String(100);
userName: String(200);
email: String(200);
givenName : String(20);
familyName : String(20);

}

In order to make view in a service more detailed was applied the following view (contains more then that fields)
entity Cases as
select from casemanager.Case {
roles[roleName = 'REQUESTER'].user as requesters,
roles[roleName = 'MAINTENANCE_TECHNICIAN'].user as maintenanceTechnicians,
}

My assumption was that it will return an array of users but returns one entity. But when I try to edit it like 'roles[roleName = 'REQUESTER'] as requesterRoles,' it works correctly and returns a list.

May be you can provide some workaround on that or is it a bug to report?
Thank you in advance!

entry.title: SAP Build Apps: Inline Frame remove sandbox attribute
entry.content:
Hi all,

i want to create an app, where visitors can do a "self" check-in.

Therefore they have to read a pdf-file for safety instructions.
The Browser runs on Kiosk mode, so the modal PDF-Preview is not that great for my use case.

Thats why I've created a small http server which hosts the pdf-file localy to display it.

In my SAP Build App I tried to display the pdf via the Inline Frame (iFrame), but Chrome is everytime saying, that it is blocked by Chrome:

"This site was blocked by Chrome"

I found out, that this has to do with the sandbox attribute of the HTML iFrame.
It sets the sandbox attribute everytime, as well when I'm not setting anything:

It also not works, when I am adding every value:
allow-downloads allow-forms allow-modals allow-orientation-lock allow-pointer-lock allow-popups allow-popups-to-escape-sandbox allow-presentation allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-storage-access-by-user-activation Experimental allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation allow-top-navigation-to-custom-protocols

When I remove the sandbox attribute by myself in the developer console, and right clicking "reload frame", it is showing my pdf like it should 🙂

 

Is there a way to remove the attribute? Or a workaround for that?

 

Thanks,

Vincent

entry.title: Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 in the area of Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management. These are the topics that are covered: 

  • Staffing Resources in Enterprise Projects Using SAP Project and Resource Management (4RF*)  
  • End-to-End Project Cost Visibility: Enhancements in Project Cost Report (1NT*) 
  • Maintain WBS Element Milestones (1NT*) 

Check out my video to get a glimpse of the new features: 

The world of project management is constantly evolving, with new tools and software updates enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of project delivery. With the latest release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, enterprise project managers and financial controllers are in for a treat. Two standout enhancements are set to revolutionize the way project costs and milestones are managed: the Project Cost Report enhancements and the ability to maintain milestones at the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element level. Moreover, we’re providing an integration with SAP Project and Resource Management which allows for the staffing of resources in enterprise projects using an external resource management solution.  

Let's dive into these features and explore their benefits for users. 

 

Staffing Resources in Enterprise Projects Using SAP Project and Resource Management  

In today's fast-paced business environment, efficient resource management is crucial for the success of enterprise projects. Utilizing an external resource management solution can significantly streamline the staffing process, ensuring that the right people with the right skills are assigned to the projects when needed. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition seamlessly integrates with SAP Project and Resource Management to simplify this task.  
 
Enterprise projects often have intricate staffing needs, requiring specific skills and timely availability. The integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Project and Resource Management addresses these complexities by enabling a seamless flow of resource requests. Project managers can create detailed resource requests, specifying necessary skills, availability, and relevant project timelines. Once these requests are published, resource managers take over and match resources to requests based on skill fit and availability. Leveraging visualization and filtering options as well as the AI-assisted matching of employees to projects ensures optimal staffing for projects.  

Figure 1: The integration of SAP Project and Resource Management increases transparency regarding resource utilization.   

 
Benefits  
 
1. Enhanced Flexibility: This integration supports both direct and external staffing. While direct staffing is used for internal employees associated with the same cost center, external staffing allows for broader resource planning using external solutions, regardless of cost center allocations. 
 
2. Real-Time Updates: Upon a hard booking of a resource, all staffing information is updated in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in real-time. This ensures transparency and up-to-date workforce utilization, aiding managers in making informed decisions. 
 
3. Comprehensive Visibility: Utilizing the “Manage My Timesheet” application, employees can access tasks related to their assignments, streamlining time tracking and ensuring accurate project accounting. 
 
The integration with SAP Project and Resource Management can significantly optimize resource staffing for enterprise projects. By leveraging this integration, enterprises can achieve a higher level of efficiency and accuracy in managing their project teams, ultimately leading to successful project outcomes. For detailed configuration guidance, users should refer to the SAP Help Portal and specific user guides available for SAP Project and Resource Management. 

If you want to find out about more highlights in this area, please check out the latest blog post by my colleague Katharina Stopf.   

 

 

 
End-to-End Project Cost Visibility 

In large-scale enterprise projects, maintaining clear visibility over project costs is crucial. Traditionally, project cost reports primarily focused on commitment costs directly associated with the project's consumption. This left a gap in understanding the ongoing and real-time insights related to project stock commitment costs and inventory-related costs that were in project stock but not yet consumed. 

With the latest enhancements in the Project Cost Report, we’re closing the last gaps in achieving end-to-end project cost visibility. The inclusion of two new columns – one for commitments related to project stock and one for inventory costs – brings a more comprehensive picture to project managers and financial controllers.  

Take a look at the demo to find out more.  

Figure 2: Providing full visibility into all cost types throughout the project’s execution cycle.   

 
Benefits  

  1. Holistic View of Project Stock Commitment Costs: The updated report provides insights into commitment costs that are tied to project stock, allowing project managers to anticipate future expenses better and plan accordingly. 
  2. Visibility of Inventory-related Costs: Users can now track inventory costs for materials not yet issued to the project. This feature helps in forecasting project expenses and ensuring resources are adequately allocated where needed. 
  3. Aggregated and non-aggregated Views: Depending on the analysis requirements, users can opt for either a detailed or summary view of the costs, offering flexibility in reporting. 

Figure 3: Two new columns as part of the Project Cost Report allow for insights into project stock commitment costs and inventory-related costs.   

 
These enhancements offer users greater transparency and control over project finances. By having all relevant costs visible in a single report, financial discrepancies can be minimized, and more accurate and timely project-financial decisions can be made. This improvement ultimately aids in budgeting accuracy and effective financial resource management, aligning with an organization's strategic financial goals. 
 

 

 

Maintain WBS Element Milestones 

Tracking project progress via milestones is key in ensuring timely project completion. However, managing milestones solely at the project level can often mean missing out on the granularity and detailed tracking needed for larger projects that use multiple WBS elements. 
 

With the introduction of milestones at the WBS element level, users can now create, manage, and track milestones specific to smaller components of the project.  

Figure 4: When maintaining milestones at WBS element level, they can be flagged as “Main Milestone” if it’s essential for the project planning. 

Benefits

  1. Track Progress at a Granular Level: By creating milestones for each WBS element, project managers can monitor the progress of each project component more precisely. 
  2. Flexibility and Precision in Planning: Milestones in the WBS element allow for more precise scheduling and resource allocation, ensuring each project component meets its deadlines. 
  3. Enhanced Project Tracking and Reporting: With milestones now visible at finer levels, the project progress and status become clearer and can be reported with greater accuracy. 
     
    The ability to define milestones at the WBS level enhances the foresight and control project managers have over a project's lifecycle. By providing clear checkpoints at various stages of the project, potential issues can be identified and resolved earlier, ensuring projects stay on track. This feature is particularly beneficial for large, complex projects where detailed oversight is crucial for success. 
     

 
The latest enhancements in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management are set to revolutionize the way projects are managed. With more detailed insights into project costs and the ability to track progress at the WBS level, these features empower project managers and financial controllers with the tools they need to optimize project execution, ensure budget adherence, and achieve strategic project goals. With the integration of SAP Project and Resource Management, resource managers are equipped with optimal staffing requirements for enterprise projects providing a higher level of efficiency and accuracy in managing project teams 

 

 

Topics Under Discovery 

To improve the offerings we have at SAP, we have a program in place which looks at topics that were raised as part of the Customer Engagement Initiative. Here we currently have two under discovery for which our customers had the biggest interest and for which we’d like to get your feedback.  

  1. Automated Planned Costs and Revenues  

The first topic focuses on automated planned costs and revenues. Currently, users must manually upload costs and revenues using tools like SAC planning or Excel sheets into the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. The aim is to enhance this process by deriving planned costs from logistics data and sales orders, enabling a streamlined automatic integration. 
 
     2. Settlement from Projects with Revenue​ 

The second topic of discovery involves the settlement of projects with revenue. Presently, only overhead and investment projects have settlement capabilities. However, there has been interest from clients to extend this functionality to revenue-generating projects as well. The exploration here involves understanding of customer requirements for both simple and complex settlements, including revenue recognition impacts.  

 

Please reach out if you want to receive more information as we aim to incorporate feedback from experienced project financial controllers and managers to steadily improve our offerings. 

 

 

 

The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now!  

The 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future. 

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners): 

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration.  
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys. 

 

 

More Information 

If you are interested in the latest and greatest blog posts on Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management, please have a look at my collection blog and also take a look at the Community page for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management (Project Control). 

 

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!    

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience – anywhere, anytime.   

   

🔎Find All of Our Assets    

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Special Edition on Data Federation and the KSA Data Center
entry.content:
Managing global supply chains has become a challenging task in the context of increased costs and disruptions. Transparency and effective communication along the supply chain have become invaluable. Hence, businesses have more and more applied could solutions to transact through global digital networks, extend visibility and establish resilience across their operations.

As you might have learned from Joern Keller’s recent blog on ‘Data Federation: Bringing New Meaning to the Adage “Think Globally, Act Locally”’, we brought our network to the next level, turning it into a distributed, decentralized, and federated network. Data federation has been enabled for our solutions SAP Business Network for Logistics and SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration. And the solutions are now also available on KSA (Kingdom of Saudi Arabia) data center SA30.

This blog will dive into the details. I will compare before- and after-scenarios, describe the new scenarios that are now possible with the availability on the KSA data center, and explain how shippers/solution owners/asset operators (hereinafter called shippers) and carriers/data contributors/OEMs and contractors (hereinafter called carriers) can manage data federation.

But first, let’s get on the same page …

 

What is data federation?

Data federation provides operational information to be accessed and updated as though it resided in a single data center. It ensures availability across regions and data centers with high scalability, and performance.

SAP Business Network for Logistics, which includes the solutions SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration, SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace and SAP Business Network, intelligent insights add on, as well as SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration are currently available on data centers EU10, US20, and SA30. Data federation allows businesses to collaborate across these data centers and regions, while providing local data residency.  

How does data federation change collaboration between business partners?

With the 2412 release, SAP Business Network has been modernized and turned into a decentralized, federated network. Before, shippers had to be on the same data center as their carriers. Collaboration across data centers was not supported.

 Before – collaboration between data centers was not possible (KSA data center was not yet available)

 Now, business data can be exchanged across data centers. Shippers can join from their preferred local home data center and establish collaboration with business partners (carriers) across the globally federated trading partner directory.

 After – collaboration between data centers is possible (graphic does not include scenarios with KSA data center)

What additional scenarios are possible with the KSA data center?

SAP Business Network for Logistics and SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration are now also available on KSA data center SA30 to serve public sector and Critical National Infrastructure (CNI) organizations. Shippers in the region, can now be compliant with data isolation regulation requirements.

 Collaboration scenario with KSA data center

Please note even if the carrier company is physically located in KSA, the account creation would happen in the US or EU data center, as the SA30 data center is dedicated to public sector and CNI companies.

 

How to manage data federation as shipper?

As a shipper, you can join SAP Business Network from your preferred home data center. To establish cross data center connections, you must ensure that the business profile is available in all data centers on the network.

In the ‘Manage Business Profile’ app, bring up your own profile and ‘Replicate to global regions of SAP Business Network’. Once enabled, the setting cannot be reversed. Future updates will be synchronized to all data centers. Additional details can be found on SAP Help Portal: Profile Settings | SAP Help Portal

 How to manage data federation as a shipper

To select a business partner operating in a different data center in the ‘Discover Business Partners’ app, you must first ensure that your company profile is available across all data centers.

Note: This feature is only available when the multi-cloud environment of SAP Business Technology Platform is used as integration platform.

 

How to manage data federation as a carrier?

During registration, carriers can select the data center on which they want to register their account. When the account has been created after December 16th, 2024, the profile is set to ‘public’ by default (the profile visibility for existing accounts remains unchanged).

The profile is automatically federated globally (this can be manually deselected), so they can be found by shippers in another data center.

Please note: There has been a UI change with regards to privacy settings. Carriers no longer find them in the ‘Manage Invitations’ app. To change the privacy settings, go to the ‘Manage Business Profile’ application and select ‘Options’. Select or deselect ‘Show my profile in search results’.

 How to manage data federation as a carrier.

entry.title: Re: How to do SELECT SINGLE/SELECT UP TO 1 ROWS using CDS
entry.content: A note about "Tables with more than two key fields": I think "concat(werks, lgort)" is risky. If "werks" has spaces at the end, "substring( Z_I_MARDFirstRow.firstRowKey, 1, 4 )" and "substring( Z_I_MARDFirstRow.firstRowKey, 5, 4 )" will give incorrect results.

entry.title: Re: Flush method is sap mdg fpm
entry.content: Well, if it calls the get_data multiple times it usually should be related to a specific event. You can check for the event and only interact with the ones you need. If that is not possible, you could check if the current call is raised by you own UI with parameter iv_raised_by_own_ui. If you want to get rid of some of the calls, a deep analysis and understanding of the application would be required.

entry.title: Do you talk funny? - What Stand-up Comedy and Business Presentations (should) have in common
entry.content:
I honestly never really thought about bringing together business presentations and stand-up comedy. This somehow doesn’t fit at all, was my first thought when my esteemed colleague recommended me a Google Talk by David Nihill. David Nihill himself, according to his own statement, has terrible stage anxiety. He hates speaking in front of people and he speaks incredibly fast (and that with a lovely Irish accent). The first half minute of his speech, he seems unprepared, hands in his pockets, and just prattles away. All in all, not exactly the classic well-trained stage speaker.

 

“You never overcome your fear of Public Speaking.”

Nevertheless, after those awkward thirty seconds, his speech captivates me. He tells a story. It is his story and how he overcomes his own limits. About how he—the man who was famous for his stage fails during his time at university— went abroad, pretended to be a comedian and by that became a stage performer. Despite this, he says, he has never overcome the fear of public speaking. He simply learned to manage it.

 

Correlation between stand-Up Comedy and business presentations

David Nihill manages his fear via stand-up comedy techniques. This not only makes his talk incredibly entertaining, but also memorable. His techniques are easy to use and don’t miss their effect: I was able to reduce the number of filler words in my presentations to zero with his tip.

So, if you also have “pigeons in your chest” instead of butterflies, or even finish your presentations with Q&As instead of a conclusion (a major mistake, he proclaims), David Nihill’s Google Talk is definitely worth a look!

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 HANA iDoc CREDAT and CRETIM format changes sending via ALE
entry.content: If you connect through RFC, then the receiver function doesn't receive dates and times in YYYY-MM-DD and HH:MM:SS formats, the RFC function receives in the RFC protocol formats (if it's ABAP, it's types D and T which correspond to YYYYMMDD and HHMMSS). Please clarify where you see YYYY-MM-DD and HH:MM:SS formats, and what's the exact chain between the RFC call and where you see these formats.

entry.title: Re: material availability date is before sales order crea...
entry.content:
Hello @Jayd_SAP 

See the following notes:

3551399 - ATP Confirmation Date in the Past for Sales Orders

The issue arises when there are no 'X' in the field DlvSchdlg and TranspSchd flag in transaction OVLY for order type.

 

2221849 - Customizing and process flow of SD scheduling

Material Availability date
In case of backwards scheduling: material availability date = loading date – pick pack time
In case of forwards scheduling: material availability date = loading date – pick pack time or current date

 

If all else fails, debug according to the note 2220102 - Debugging guide for SD scheduling

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: How to use I_ProductTP_2 in mock-framework?
entry.content:
Hi,

i have got a functionality that at some point in time also updates the material master using MODIFY ENTITY ... UPDATE on I_ProductTP_2. 

I want to get that functionality under automated test, but I don't seem to be able to actually mock the data in a way that it can be updated.

This is what I've done:

 

METHOD class_setup.
 sql_test_environment = cl_osql_test_environment=>create( i_dependency_list = VALUE #( ( 'I_ProductTP_2' ) ) ).
ENDMETHOD.
METHOD my_test.
 TYPES tty_product_ids_wr TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF I_ProductTP_2 WITH DEFAULT KEY.
 sql_test_environment->insert_test_data( i_data = VALUE tty_product_ids_wr( ( Product = '990000000000000001' ) ) ).
 sql_test_environment->insert_test_data( i_data = VALUE tty_product_ids_wr( ( Product = '990000000000000002' ) ) ).
ENDMETHOD.

 

So If I now SELECT from I_ProductTP_2, then my inserted products are perfectly there, but if I run the following, I get a "404 not found" error:

 

MODIFY ENTITY I_ProductTP_2
  UPDATE FIELDS ( ProductOldID )
  WITH VALUE #( ( %key-Product       = '990000000000000001'
                  %data-ProductOldID = '990000000000000002' ) )
  FAILED FINAL(failed) ##NEEDED
  REPORTED FINAL(reported) ##NEEDED.

 

So: what am I doing wrong here? If I try to use the CDS-Test-Environment for I_ProductTP_2 the test crashes because one of the underlying CDSes if I_ProductTP_2 must not be used with the CDS-Test-Environment for some unexplained reason.

To me this looks pretty broken on SAP-side, but maybe I am just missing out something here?

entry.title: Re: Individual Purchase order
entry.content:
Hi,

For Individual Purchase order

1.Item category group is BANC

2. Item category is TAB --> TAB determined based on 

            Sales Doc type - OR

            Item category group - BANC

             Item Usage - NIL

            Higher-level item category - NIL

Here we have Special Stock indicator - E  : It controls when you doing delivery system will consider Sales Order Stock.

3. Schedule line Category - CB (Determined based on Item Category + MRP Type)

 in CB we have few controls 

A. Movement Type - 601

B. Item Relevant for delivery check box

C. Order type - NB --> it will help to generate PR automatically in the background when you save the order.

D. Item Category - 0 --> If you do MIGO it consider as actual MIGO and stocks will be updated.

E. Account assignment category - E --> even if you do delivery system will not generate inventory document and cost of goods will be directly assign to that particular sales order number

F. Purchase requestion delivery schedule check box - it will help to copy the schedule line data sales order to PR

4. Requirement Type - KEB

5. Requirement class - KEB - here we have few controls

A. Special stock indicator - E - Stock will reserved to that particular sales order number

B. Account assignment category - E-->even if you do MIGO system will not generate inventory document and cost of goods will be directly assign to that particular sales order number

Regards,

Rajasekhar

entry.title: Re: Error during import of clipboard contents
entry.content:
As already answered in the forum many times, this function module can't work in background, because it's based on OLE to connect to the Excel application installed on the user's laptop, and in background it's impossible to connect to the user's laptop (like e.g. impossible to use GUI_UPLOAD, etc.)

Depending on the Excel file format, there are several workarounds:

  • If it's a text format like CSV or tab-separated fields, you may upload the file to the application server before running the job, and you use OPEN DATASET, READ DATASET, etc. to read the text lines and fields.
  • For other formats, you may use any solution like abap2xlsx or XCO for XLSX format, custom XML transformations if it's an XML-like format, etc.

entry.title: Re: SAP ByDesign Api - Upload Attachment
entry.content:
Can you check if the following works for you?

<soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope" xmlns:glob="http://sap.com/xi/SAPGlobal20/Global" xmlns:a00="http://sap.com/xi/AP/CustomerExtension/BYD/A0014" xmlns:glob1="http://sap.com/xi/AP/Globalization">
<soap:Header/>
<soap:Body>
<glob:MaterialBundleMaintainRequest_sync_V1>
<BasicMessageHeader></BasicMessageHeader>
<!--1 or more repetitions:-->
<Material actionCode="06">
<InternalID>P100001</InternalID>
<AttachmentFolder DocumentListCompleteTransmissionIndicator="false" ActionCode="06">
<Document PropertyListCompleteTransmissionIndicator="false" ActionCode="01">
<VisibleIndicator>true</VisibleIndicator>
<!--Optional:-->
<CategoryCode>2</CategoryCode>
<!--Optional
<TypeCode>10011</TypeCode>:-->
<!--Optional
<MIMECode>text/plain</MIMECode>:-->
<!--Optional:-->
<Name>Test.txt</Name>
<!--Optional:-->
<AlternativeName>Test.txt</AlternativeName>
<!--Optional:-->
<FileContent ActionCode="01">
<!--Optional:-->
<BinaryObject mimeCode="text/plain" characterSetCode="UTF?">*Base64BinaryString*</BinaryObject>
</FileContent>
</Document>
</AttachmentFolder>
</Material>
</glob:MaterialBundleMaintainRequest_sync_V1>
</soap:Body>
</soap:Envelope>
